evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan Count to my .NET for iOS app. Here's my empty view controller: EmptyViewController.cs. I need to count EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes, show the built-in counting UI, and when the user taps the List button store the scanned barcodes so I can show them on a results screen.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit NuGet versions, writes complete BarcodeCount integration code into EmptyViewController.cs: DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey, a manually-managed Camera (Camera.GetDefaultCamera() + camera.ApplySettingsAsync(BarcodeCount.RecommendedCameraSettings) + dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync), new BarcodeCountSettings() enabling only Ean13Upca and Code128, BarcodeCount.Create(context, settings), BarcodeCountView.Create(View.Bounds, context, barcodeCount[, style]) added via View.AddSubview, barcodeCount.Scanned event (or AddListener) that copies session.RecognizedBarcodes out via InvokeOnMainThread, ListButtonTapped handler storing the barcodes, camera SwitchToDesiredStateAsync On/Standby + barcodeCount.Enabled across ViewWillAppear/ViewWillDisappear. Shows the setup checklist (NuGet packages, Info.plist NSCameraUsageDescription, AppDelegate initialize for SDK 8.0+, SupportedOSPlatformVersion 15.0, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding the Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions calling ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() and ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() in AppDelegate.FinishedLaunching (required for SDK 8.0+)
Setup checklist mentions setting SupportedOSPlatformVersion to at least 15.0
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
new BarcodeCountSettings() is used (the settings use a plain constructor)
BarcodeCount.Create( is used as the factory (NOT new BarcodeCount(...), which has no public constructor)
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
Native-style symbology names (e.g. Symbology.ean13UPCA, .code128) are NOT used
A Camera is obtained via Camera.GetDefaultCamera() (or Camera.DefaultCamera) and BarcodeCount.RecommendedCameraSettings is applied via camera.ApplySettingsAsync(...)
dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync( is called with the camera (the camera is managed explicitly, not by the view)
camera.SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On) and FrameSourceState.Standby (or .Off) are used to drive the camera across the lifecycle
barcodeCount.Enabled is set to true so frames are processed
BarcodeCountView.Create( is called (not new BarcodeCountView(...), which is not a public constructor)
BarcodeCountView.Create( receives a CGRect frame (e.g. this.View.Bounds) as its first argument (NOT an Android Context)
The BarcodeCountView is added to the view hierarchy via this.View.AddSubview(...) (the view IS a UIView)
Camera lifecycle is driven from ViewWillAppear / ViewWillDisappear (iOS view controller lifecycle), not Android OnResume/OnPause
No barcodeCountView.OnResume() / OnPause() / Start() / Stop() calls are made (those do not exist on BarcodeCountView)
Either barcodeCount.Scanned += handler or barcodeCount.AddListener(IBarcodeCountListener) is wired
The scan handler reads args.Session.RecognizedBarcodes (the BarcodeCountSession exposes RecognizedBarcodes, not AddedTrackedBarcodes)

The recognized barcodes are copied out of the session (e.g. .ToList()) inside the callback, and UI work is dispatched onto the main thread via UIApplication.SharedApplication.InvokeOnMainThread (or DispatchQueue.MainQueue), NOT Android's RunOnUiThread
A barcodeCountView.ListButtonTapped handler is wired to store/show the scanned barcodes
No Android-only APIs are used (no Android Context, no AddView, no RunOnUiThread, no AppCompatActivity, no AndroidManifest)
If the listener interface is used, OnObservationStarted and OnObservationStopped are implemented (IBarcodeCountListener has three methods)
No BarcodeArView, IBarcodeArHighlightProvider, or BarcodeAr API is used (MatrixScan Count is not MatrixScan AR)
No DataCaptureView or BarcodeCaptureOverlay is created (BarcodeCountView is the counting UI surface)
No MAUI XAML or Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui package is introduced (this is a non-MAUI iOS app)

evals.prompt: I'm building a receiving app with MatrixScan Count on .NET iOS. I already have it counting (see IntegratedViewController.cs). Now I want to check scanned barcodes against an expected list: 3x of 0123456789012 and 1x of 9876543210987, and react when everything on the list has been scanned.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds a capture list: builds TargetBarcode.Create(data, quantity) entries, creates a BarcodeCountCaptureList.Create(listener, targets), implements IBarcodeCountCaptureListListener (OnObservationStarted/OnObservationStopped/OnCaptureListSessionUpdated/OnCaptureListCompleted), and applies it via barcodeCount.SetBarcodeCountCaptureList(list), preserving existing integration.

assertions.text: TargetBarcode.Create(data, quantity) is used to build the expected barcodes (factory, not new TargetBarcode(...))
A TargetBarcode for 0123456789012 with quantity 3 and one for 9876543210987 with quantity 1 are created
BarcodeCountCaptureList.Create(listener, targets) is used (factory, not new BarcodeCountCaptureList(...))
IBarcodeCountCaptureListListener is implemented with OnObservationStarted, OnObservationStopped, OnCaptureListSessionUpdated, and OnCaptureListCompleted
barcodeCount.SetBarcodeCountCaptureList(list) is called to apply the list
OnCaptureListCompleted is where the all-scanned reaction is handled
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler, ViewWillAppear/ViewWillDisappear) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration in my MatrixScan Count .NET iOS app — silent counting. Update the feedback in IntegratedViewController.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCount.Feedback to a new BarcodeCountFeedback() (empty constructor = silent), imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Count.Feedback, preserving the rest of the integration. It does NOT call DefaultFeedback as a method.

assertions.text: barcodeCount.Feedback is reassigned to a new BarcodeCountFeedback() (empty constructor)
BarcodeCountFeedback is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Count.Feedback
BarcodeCountFeedback.DefaultFeedback is NOT called as a method (with parentheses) — it is a static property
The feedback slots are referred to as Success / Failure if mentioned (NOT Scanned / Tapped, which are MatrixScan AR's)
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: My labels in this MatrixScan Count .NET iOS app (IntegratedViewController.cs) carry both Code 128 and PDF417 barcodes, but I only want to count the Code 128 ones. Filter out PDF417 so it's never counted, and also exclude any barcode whose data starts with 1234.
evals.expected_output: The skill configures BarcodeCountSettings.FilterSettings: sets FilterSettings.ExcludedSymbologies to a set containing Symbology.Pdf417 and FilterSettings.ExcludedCodesRegex to a regex matching data starting with 1234, preserving the existing integration. FilterSettings is read-only on BarcodeCountSettings (mutate its members, do not reassign).

assertions.text: settings.FilterSettings.ExcludedSymbologies is set to a collection containing Symbology.Pdf417
settings.FilterSettings.ExcludedCodesRegex is set to a regex string that matches barcode data starting with 1234
The PascalCase symbology name Symbology.Pdf417 is used, NOT the native style Symbology.pdf417
FilterSettings is accessed off the BarcodeCountSettings instance and its members are mutated, NOT reassigned (it is a read-only property)
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count .NET iOS app (IntegratedViewController.cs) I'm filtering out some barcodes. The filtered barcodes are currently transparent — I want to highlight them with a visible colored brush instead of leaving them invisible.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns barcodeCountView.FilterSettings to a BarcodeFilterHighlightSettingsBrush built with the static BarcodeFilterHighlightSettingsBrush.Create(Brush) factory wrapping a new Brush(...). It uses the view's FilterSettings (the highlight), distinct from the settings' FilterSettings (the rules), preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.FilterSettings is assigned to control the filtered-barcode highlight
BarcodeFilterHighlightSettingsBrush.Create( is used as the factory to build the highlight settings
new BarcodeFilterHighlightSettingsBrush( is NOT present
A Brush is constructed and passed to the BarcodeFilterHighlightSettingsBrush.Create factory
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count .NET iOS app (IntegratedViewController.cs) every barcode value in the scene is guaranteed unique — no duplicates. Configure it to take advantage of that for faster scanning.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.ExpectsOnlyUniqueBarcodes = true on the BarcodeCountSettings before creating the mode, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: settings.ExpectsOnlyUniqueBarcodes is set to true on the BarcodeCountSettings
settings.ExpectsOnlyUniqueBarcodes = true is present (semantic)
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count .NET iOS app (IntegratedViewController.cs) I want the count to survive backgrounding: when the app goes to the background save the currently scanned barcodes as already-counted, and provide a way to clear them and start a brand-new counting process.
evals.expected_output: The skill calls barcodeCount.SetAdditionalBarcodes(IList<Barcode>) to seed the previously scanned barcodes as already-counted (e.g. on UIApplication.DidEnterBackgroundNotification), and uses barcodeCount.ClearAdditionalBarcodes() together with barcodeCount.Reset() to start fresh, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: barcodeCount.SetAdditionalBarcodes( is called to seed the already-counted barcodes
barcodeCount.ClearAdditionalBarcodes() is called to clear the seeded barcodes
barcodeCount.Reset() is called to start a brand-new counting process
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count .NET iOS app (IntegratedViewController.cs) I want to customize the AR overlay colors — color recognized barcodes with my own brush. I'm using the Dot style.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns barcodeCountView.Listener to an IBarcodeCountViewListener implementation that returns custom Brush instances from BrushForRecognizedBarcode (and the other brush-for callbacks), implementing all nine iOS methods, preserving the existing integration. It does NOT use the Android-only OnCaptureListCompleted on the view listener.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.Listener is assigned an IBarcodeCountViewListener implementation
BrushForRecognizedBarcode returns a custom Brush to color recognized barcodes
The view listener implements the brush-for callbacks BrushForRecognizedBarcodeNotInList, BrushForAcceptedBarcode, and BrushForRejectedBarcode
The view listener implements the tap callbacks OnRecognizedBarcodeTapped, OnFilteredBarcodeTapped, OnRecognizedBarcodeNotInListTapped, OnAcceptedBarcodeTapped, and OnRejectedBarcodeTapped
OnCaptureListCompleted is NOT implemented on the view listener (semantic) (that method is Android-only)
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: My MatrixScan Count .NET iOS receiving app (IntegratedViewController.cs) already checks against an expected list. When the user scans a barcode that's NOT on the list, I want to prompt them to accept or reject it, with custom Accept and Reject button labels.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables the not-in-list action via barcodeCountView.BarcodeNotInListActionSettings: sets Enabled = true and customizes AcceptButtonText / RejectButtonText (and optionally the accepted/rejected hints), preserving the existing integration. BarcodeNotInListActionSettings is read off the view (mutate its members, do not construct it).

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.BarcodeNotInListActionSettings is accessed off the view
The not-in-list action Enabled property is set to true
AcceptButtonText and RejectButtonText are set to custom strings
BarcodeNotInListActionSettings members are mutated, NOT a new BarcodeCountNotInListActionSettings(...) constructed (semantic) (it is read off the view)
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count .NET iOS app (IntegratedViewController.cs) I want to use Status Mode to annotate each counted barcode as low stock, and show the Status Mode button in the UI.
evals.expected_output: The skill implements IBarcodeCountStatusProvider (OnStatusRequested builds BarcodeCountStatusItem.Create(barcode, BarcodeCountStatus.LowStock) for each tracked barcode and calls callback.OnStatusReady with BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess.Create(...)), registers it via barcodeCountView.SetStatusProvider(provider), and sets barcodeCountView.ShouldShowStatusModeButton = true, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: An IBarcodeCountStatusProvider is implemented with an OnStatusRequested method
barcodeCountView.SetStatusProvider( is called to register the status provider
barcodeCountView.ShouldShowStatusModeButton is set to true
BarcodeCountStatus.LowStock is used as the per-barcode status
BarcodeCountStatusItem.Create( is used to build each status item
The provider callback delivers results via BarcodeCountStatusResultSuccess.Create(
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count .NET iOS app (IntegratedViewController.cs) I want a minimal UI: hide the List, Exit, and Shutter buttons, and turn off the user guidance view and hints.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the BarcodeCountView ShouldShow* toggles: ShouldShowListButton = false, ShouldShowExitButton = false, ShouldShowShutterButton = false, ShouldShowUserGuidanceView = false, ShouldShowHints = false, preserving the existing integration.

assertions.text: barcodeCountView.ShouldShowListButton is set to false
barcodeCountView.ShouldShowExitButton is set to false
barcodeCountView.ShouldShowShutterButton is set to false
barcodeCountView.ShouldShowUserGuidanceView is set to false
barcodeCountView.ShouldShowHints is set to false
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan Count .NET iOS app (IntegratedViewController.cs) I want a 'Start Over' action that clears all counted barcodes and AR overlays so the user can begin a fresh counting process.
evals.expected_output: The skill calls barcodeCount.Reset() to clear all counted barcodes and AR overlays for a fresh scanning process, preserving the existing integration. Reset() is called on the BarcodeCount mode, not on the session outside the callback.

assertions.text: barcodeCount.Reset() is called to clear counted barcodes and AR overlays
Reset() is called on the BarcodeCount mode instance
Existing integration code (camera, BarcodeCount.Create, BarcodeCountView, Scanned handler) is preserved
